parsing_res_list.block_content: The Economist Marchgth2024
The Americas

one week before the 2018election. Lula's favoured candidate lost to Jair Bolsonaro,a hard-rightpopulist twhose campaign e been boosted by anti-establishment t sentiment generated by LavaJato.Mr Morot thenleft judiciary tobecome Mr Bolsonaros justice minister. The lead prosecutor,T DeltanI Dallagnol,-became a congressman for a right-wing party. In 2019 leaked text messages published by The Intercept, an investigative site,suggested that Mr Moro had been colluding on cases withMr Dallagnol.Both men told The Economist there was no collusion,and pointed out that re majority of judicial decisions on Lava Jato were initially 7upheld on appeal. Mr Dallagnol said the task force did ou recognise the authenticity of the leaked messages,Mr Moro that they had 'never been examined in open court".

Ta Critics also accused the Lava Jato taskforce of using aggressive tactics to court media attention. Lula was arrested in 2016using a bench warrant,usually used when a subpoenaed person refuses to attend a hearing, something he had not done. In Peru and Brazil, prosecutors were criticised for the extensive use of pre-trial detention.uo Brazilian investigator forbade the rector of university from entering his campus,and briefly imprisoned dhim non suspicion of corruption.ue rector committed suicide soon after.He wasinnocent.

Politicians pounced on these cracks."Lava Jato was like the Jacobin moment of the French Revolution,"0says Marco Bastos of Southern T Pulse,a consultancy.Brazilians gawked at their televisions as a parade ofpoliticians were figurativelyguillotined.Then the old guard struck back.
Dvinglight

because they felt she was doingtoo little to protect them. Romero Juca, a senator, was caught t on a wiretap before her impeachment,tellingapoliticalally:"This shit has to be fixed..the government needs to be changed to stanch the bleeding." He said his words had been taken out of context.

In 2017 Ms Rousseff's successor, Michel Temer, who was imprisoned for graft himself and repeatedly dismissed the accusations as lies,pardoned non-violent offendcee jailed d for corruption who had served one-fifth of their sentence.His decree claimed that this could curb the"exponential growth of the prison population". In 2019 the Supreme Court reversed an earlier decision nandruled that defendants be allowedtoe exhaust all avenues for appeal before being jailed.That freed Lula and protected manypoliticians from prison. Conseee passed da law criminalising"abuses"by prosecutors and judges sin the same year, with a broad definition of abuse.

Mr Temer's successor, Mr Bolsonaro,hadhis own reasons to break with Lava Jato,despite having ridden the wave of sentiment it created to power. He appointed an attorney general who threw out over 100requests to investigate Mr Bolsonaro.In 2020,when Lava Jato began 1scrutinising one of his sons,who is also a politician, Mr Bolsonaro was swift tto declare that t"there is no more corruption in government."(Hiss son denies wrongdoing,and calls the investigations "politicalp persecution".He disbanded r Lava Jato task force in 2021.Central America's anti-corruption crusaders met similar fate.Internationally
Spend money to make money
Selected Foreign Corrupt Practices Act cases
Bribes paid, $m Gross profits from bribes, $m Total fine, $m
Bribes paid to:Angola,Argentina,Brazil,Colombia Dominican Republic,Ecuador,Guatemala,Mexico,MozambiquePanama,Peru,Venezuela Fines paidto: Brazil, Switzerland,United States
Bribes paid to:Argentina, Bangladesh,China, Iaq Israel,Mexico, Nigeria, Russia,Venezuela, Vietnam Fines paid to: Germany, United States
Bribes paid to: Libya
Fines paid to: France,United States
Source:Thewaysofcorruption ininfrastructure lessons fromtheOdebrechtcasebyN.Camposetal,2021

the backlash against anti-corruption efforts 1continues.The judge himself has been linked to Lava Jato.In 2019Crusoe,e Brazilian investigative outlet,published an article about emails sent by Odebrecht's boss in 2007 which referred to Mr Toffoli,then the attorney general,as "the friend of my father's friend"The article alleged that "my father's friend" was a reference to Lula, who was president at the time. He appointed Mr Tofoli to the Supreme Court in 2009.After the story was published, the Supreme Court deemed the article "fake news" and ordered it be scrubbed from the internet. Only p public coutcry forced the decision to be reversed. Along with suspending fines to be paid by Odebrecht and J&F,Mr Toffoli has also annulled all evidence gathered in Odebrecht's leniency agreement, He declined to comment.

Few people in Brazil want to talk about corruption anymore, except to express disdain for Lava Jato.Gilmar Mendes,a Supreme Court judge,dismisses it as the product of foreign interference, "propaganda"by the media,and "anti-corruption fighters [who] like money a lot".
Across the aisle

Theblowbackon Lava Jato is bipartisan.Mr Moro,nowa senator,faces twotrials which could bar him from offi ce.One,concerning alleged campaign-finance abuses,was filed by Mr Bolsonaros party. The other,which alleges that Mr Moro committed fraud as part of an old plea deal, was launched by Mr Toffoli.Mr Dallagnol lost his seat in Congress after a decision by an electoral court on a t technicality.He notes that the deciding judge was himself investigated by Lava Jato,and that lower courts had ruled in his favour.Mr Moros says both accusations against him are baseless.

On February 26th a different judge on Brazil's Supreme Court authorised companies that had signed leniency agreements during Lava Jato to renegotiate the terms.They were given 09days to do so, during which time allfi nesrelated tothe case have been suspended.T The companies claimed they felt coerced into signing the agreements, which "jeopardise their existence".
E The undoing of Lava Jato has resonated across Latin America.uI Peru,former offcials arep pointing to the annulment of the Odebrecht evidence as part of their efforts to have their cases thrown out.

The ancien régime has been fighting back, and 1it has been winning.Butit should be careful.In a national pollreleased on March 3rd a plurality of Brazilians said 1Lava Jato was shut down due to political interests.Fully 274%of those polled believe that the Supreme Court's recent decisions "encourage corruption".
